California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 5, 1989 
Lance H. Olson, Esq. 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
Re: 
Your Request for Confirmation 
of Telephone Advice 
Our File No. A-89-271 
This is to confirm as accurate your summary of the telephone 
advice we provided to you on April 25, 1989 concerning the 
contribution limits under the Political Reform Act (the "Act") 1/ 
Specifically, we informed you that, based upon Regulation 
18500, (copy enclosed), contributions made to a candidate between 
July 1, 1988 and December 31, 1988 do not count toward the Act's 
contribution limits for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1989. 
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5901. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General~ounsel

1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


LANCE H. OLSON 
BRUCE J. HAGEL 
LEROY Y. FONG 
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH 
OF COUNSEL 
LLOYD C. CONNELLY, Member 
California State Legislature 
~oo Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
iacramento, California 95814 
TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952 
FAX: (916) 442-1280 
Law Offices of 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
May 4, 1989 
Mr. Scott Hallabrin 
Counsel, Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices commission 
428 J street, suite 800 
Sacramento, California 95814 
S 
'89 
RE: 
Request for written Confirmation of Telephone 
Advice 
Dear Mr. Hallabrin: 
The purpose of this letter is to confirm advice 
given to me by you in our telephone conversation of 
Tuesday, April 25, 1989. 
As you will recall I telephoned to follow-up on 
advice given by you in your letter of March 21, 1989 
(Advice Letter No. A-89-100). 
As you recall following 
the Los Angeles Superior Court's ruling we had 
submitted a series of questions on behalf of certain 
specified clients of this law firm. 
In our telephone 
conversation of April 25th I sought follow-up advice on 
behalf of those same clients and those similarly 
situated concerning the following questions: 
Assume that a candidate, pursuant to 
Commission regulations in effect until 
February 8, 1989, placed contributions 
received prior to January 1, 1989 in a 
campaign bank account and intended to use the 
contributions to support the candidate's 
election to future office. 
Also assume that 
those contributions were then commingled in 
the campaign bank account with contributions 
received on or after January 1, 1989. 
Assume further that funds from the commingled 
campaign account were expended in excess of 
the amounts received between January 1, 1989 
and February 8, 1989. 
Are contributions made 
to the candidate between July 1, 1988 and 
December 31, 1988 counted towards the 
contribution limits applicable to the fiscal 
year ending June 30, 1989 for contributions 
received by the candidate after February 8, 
1989. 


May 4, 1989 
Page Two 
Based on the above situation you advised me that 
contributions made to a candidate between July 1, 1988 and 
December 31, 1988 are not counted towards the contribution limits 
applicable to the fiscal year ending June 30, 1989 for 
contributions made after February 8, 1989. 
This advice was based 
upon emergency regulation 18500 adopted by the Commission at its 
April 4, 1989 meeting. 
If I have misstated your advice is any way please notify me 
immediately. 
otherwise you may provide a written response in the 
normal course. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON

95814 
RE: 
Request for written Confirmation of Telephone 
Advice 
Dear Mr. Hallabrin: 
The purpose of this letter is to confirm advice 
given to me by you in our telephone conversation of 
Tuesday, April 25, 1989. 
As you will recall I telephoned to follow-up on 
advice given by you in your letter of March 21, 1989 
(Advice Letter No. A-89-100). 
As you recall following 
the Los Angeles Superior Court's ruling we had 
submitted a series of questions on behalf of certain 
specified clients of this law firm. 
In our telephone 
conversation of April 25th I sought follow-up advice on 
behalf of those same clients and those similarly 
situated concerning the following questions: 
Assume that a candidate, pursuant to 
Commission regulations in effect until 
February 8, 1989, placed contributions 
received prior to January I, 1989 in a 
campaign bank account and intended to use the 
contributions to support the candidate's 
election to future office. 
Also assume that 
those contributions were then commingled in 
the campaign bank account with contributions 
received on or after January I, 1989. 
Assume further that funds from the commingled 
campaign account were expended in excess of 
the amounts received between January I, 1989 
and February 8, 1989. 
Are contributions made 
to the candidate between July I, 1988 and 
December 31, 1988 counted towards the 
contribution limits applicable to the fiscal 
year ending June 30, 1989 for contributions 
received by the candidate after February 8, 
1989. 


May 4, 1989 
Page Two 
Based on the above situation you advised me that 
contributions made to a candidate between July 1, 1988 and 
December 31, 1988 are not counted towards the contribution limits 
applicable to the fiscal year ending June 30, 1989 for 
contributions made after February 8, 1989. 
This advice was based 
upon emergency regulation 18500 adopted by the Commission at its 
April 4, 1989 meeting. 
If I have misstated your advice is any way please notify me 
immediately. 
otherwise you may provide a written response in the 
normal course. 
Very truly yours, 
OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL & FONG 
LANCE H. OLSON 
LHO:kh 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
May 9, 1989 
Lance Olson 
Olson, Connelly, Hagel & Fong 
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350 
Sacramento, CA 
95814 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-271 
Dear Mr. Olson: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the Political Reform Act on May 8, 1989. 
Your letter has 
been assigned to Scott Hallabrin for response. 
If you have any 
questions, you may contact him directly at (916) 322-5901. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660
